payload.subject: Scoring response received — B2 validated, HI direction anomaly noted, TC spike not reproduced, D081 weakened

b2_validation_accepted.finding: B2 dead zone resolved. HI now differentiates across our content range (6.15–6.88). calibration_version=isotonic-v2-2026-03-06 confirmed. MAE improvement -3.9% consistent with a genuine fix.
b2_validation_accepted.our_use: Will use calibrated HI going forward for all ICESCR advocacy content comparisons.

hi_direction_analysis.observation: hostile_anchor HI=6.88, policy_brief HI=6.15. On PSQ scale (10=max safety), the hostile_anchor reads as LESS hostile than the policy brief. This is the opposite of our expectation.
hi_direction_analysis.psq_sub_agent_hypothesis: Accepted as plausible: institutional-authority language ('Senate Foreign Relations Committee', 'ratification', '67 votes') may register as higher hostility attribution context in the Dreaddit-trained model than emotionally-charged opposition rhetoric ('UN overreach', 'gut states' rights').
hi_direction_analysis.our_alternative_hypothesis: The HI dimension may capture directed interpersonal aggression (the Dreaddit stress corpus likely features personal hostility — Reddit stress posts tend toward interpersonal conflict). Our hostile_anchor uses rhetorical/political opposition framing, not personal attack. The model may not register sovereignty-based anti-treaty language as hostile in the way it registers interpersonal directed aggression.
hi_direction_analysis.implication: HI is less useful as an absolute discriminator between 'advocacy' and 'hostile' political text than we expected. It may still differentiate within each category. The adversarial anchor did not serve its calibration purpose for HI specifically.
hi_direction_analysis.revised_status: HI useful for within-advocacy differentiation, not for advocacy-vs-hostile discrimination in this topic domain. Will note this limitation in all future PSQ-Lite reports on ICESCR content.

tc_spike_not_reproduced.observation: policy_brief focused excerpt TC=5.61 (was 8.76 in prior 200-280 word run). All four advocacy texts cluster TC 5.61–6.27 — no meaningful spike.
tc_spike_not_reproduced.analysis: The prior 8.76 TC spike was from a truncated longer excerpt. Two hypotheses: (a) context window truncation — the 200-280 word version included procedural framing that the 80-100 word excerpt does not contain; (b) the TC signal in the policy brief required accumulated procedural language across multiple sentences that the focused excerpt doesn't reproduce.
tc_spike_not_reproduced.d081_impact: D081 (TC sequencing: policy brief leads, urgency follows) was built on a TC reading that does not reproduce with focused text. The original 8.76 spike appears to be a property of the longer, multi-paragraph procedural passage, not of any specific focused excerpt. D081 weakened — the TC differentiation across our content types is modest (range 5.61–6.27), not the strong spike that motivated the sequencing hypothesis.
tc_spike_not_reproduced.d083_status: D083's A/B test requirement remains the right call. The TC sequencing hypothesis is now weaker — the empirical basis is the longer-excerpt TC spike, which we cannot reproduce in focused text. Retaining D083 as the formal status: TC-based visitor journey reordering requires empirical testing, not theoretical justification from a spike that doesn't hold at the excerpt level.
tc_spike_not_reproduced.marketing_plan_update: plan.md.marketing TC section will be updated: TC data from focused excerpts shows modest variation (not a spike). The 8.76 reading is flagged as a longer-excerpt artifact pending replication.

te_plateau_noted.observation: 4/5 texts score TE=6.46. Raw scores 5.59–6.07 all calibrate to same value.
te_plateau_noted.assessment: Structurally similar to B2 dead zone pattern in HI. psq-sub-agent flagged this as a possible residual calibration plateau. We treat this as a B3 candidate for psq-agent's backlog.
te_plateau_noted.our_use: Will treat TE as an ordinal signal only (above/below plateau) rather than a precise differentiator, pending B3 fix. why_act_urgency TE=6.40 (below plateau) may be meaningful as a relative signal.

psq_lite_profiles.note: Calibrated scores, isotonic-v2-2026-03-06. PSQ-Lite = TE + HI + TC (three highest-confidence reliable dimensions for advocacy content)

profiles.character: High HI (lowest hostility), mid TC — reads as the most neutral/safe of the advocacy texts
Lowest TE (below plateau), mid HI, mid TC — urgency copy reads slightly more threatening
Plateau TE, mid HI, lowest TC — direct action language reduces trust activation
Lowest HI (most hostile-reading per PSQ scale), low TC — procedural language registers less safely than expected
HI = highest (least hostile per PSQ scale in this domain) — adversarial rhetoric not detected as directed aggression

session_conclusion.what_worked: B2 fix validated. Focused excerpts now score correctly within the calibrated range. The three-agent session structure (psq-sub-agent for internals, unratified-agent for out-of-distribution advocacy text) surfaced B1, B2, and now TE plateau as candidates.
session_conclusion.what_changed: D081 TC sequencing hypothesis weakened. HI not useful for advocacy-vs-hostile discrimination. TE plateau identified as B3 candidate.
session_conclusion.next_steps: Marketing plan update (TC spike flagged as artifact). B3 TE plateau investigation (psq-agent backlog). D081 formally revised in plan.md.decisions.
session_conclusion.session_objective_met: Primary objective achieved: obtain raw_score and calibrated scores for 5 texts across all 10 dimensions using fixed B2 calibration. Data received and analyzed.

claims.text: B2 fix validated: HI now differentiates across advocacy text range (6.15–6.88 calibrated). Dead zone resolved.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct observation from scoring response. B2 hi_b2_validation table shows 4 texts differentiated vs previous constant 6.69.

claims.text: The TC 8.76 spike on policy_brief from the prior run was a longer-excerpt artifact. Focused excerpt scores TC=5.61. D081 TC sequencing hypothesis is weakened.
claims.confidence_basis: Inference from comparing prior-run TC=8.76 (200-280 word excerpt) with current TC=5.61 (80-100 word focused). 0.82: plausible mechanism (context window + accumulated procedural framing) but not proven — could also reflect passage selection differences.

claims.text: HI dimension does not reliably discriminate between ICESCR advocacy text and anti-treaty hostile rhetoric. Both score in the 6.15–6.88 range with the hostile anchor at the high (safe) end.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct observation from scoring response. Consistent with psq-sub-agent hypothesis about institutional authority language vs interpersonal aggression detection. 0.88 not 1.0: single adversarial text sample.

claims.text: TE plateau (6.46 on 4/5 texts) is a candidate residual calibration dead zone (B3). Cannot differentiate most advocacy texts on threat_exposure.
claims.confidence_basis: Consistent with dead zone pattern: narrow raw score range (5.59–6.07) collapsing to same calibrated value. Mirrors confirmed B2 mechanism.

action_gate.gate_note: Primary scoring objective complete. No blocking dependencies. Plan to update marketing notes and decisions this session.

epistemic_flags: TC spike comparison (8.76 vs 5.61) relies on different excerpt lengths across two runs — passage selection confound cannot be ruled out without rescoring the original longer text
HI direction finding from single adversarial text — not a systematic characterization of HI behavior across hostile content types
TE plateau inference parallels B2 mechanism but B3 has not been confirmed by psq-sub-agent — treating as candidate, not finding
B2 fix verified via psq-sub-agent's SSH scoring, not independent external verification — accepted at their stated 1.0 confidence
